title: Enforce default accelerator conflict policy at startup

labels.name: ai-dispatch
labels.description: Queued for the AI dispatch loop

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

labels.name: ai-dispatch-retry
labels.description: AI dispatch re-queued for one retry

labels.name: ai-dispatch-failure-stall
labels.description: AI dispatch terminal failure: Codex watchdog stall

labels.name: ai-dispatch-recovered-transient
labels.description: AI dispatch one-shot transient (stall/timeout) recovery marker; do not remove

body:
Establish default-accelerator ownership and fatal startup enforcement for default accelerator conflicts only; runtime conflict handling remains non-fatal and unchanged.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-ui/src/menus/default_menu.rs`, `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`.
Done: a pure editor-ui helper panics with clear offending shortcuts on default conflicts, `EguiHost::new()` invokes it once, tests cover real-menu zero conflicts and injected duplicate, accelerator values stay unchanged at 19/zero conflicts.
MUST NOT edit per-keystroke routing, shortcut conflict display, Command/predicate logic, CAD/editor-actions/editor-state/Cargo/schemas/workflows/scripts/automation; if enforcement needs a MUST-NOT surface, record `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` instead.

**Self-re-arm (final step, required):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only
AUDIT task as task 171 â€” a "Post-keybinding-policy Phase 9 next-task source audit"
mirroring the task-169 audit block: confirm default-accelerator conflicts are fatal at
the single host startup site, the per-keystroke/non-fatal display paths are unchanged,
no accelerator value changed (table = 19, zero conflicts), and that only editor-ui
(enforcement + tests) and the editor-egui-host startup call site changed. Task 171 is
docs/source-read-only (its `MAY edit` includes `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`,
`HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; it MUST NOT edit Rust source, tests, or
automation). Task 171's final step appends the next bounded FEATURE task (or, if none is
in-policy, records a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a
"Recommendation for human approval" block). Copy this Self-re-arm requirement verbatim
into the task 171 block you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

title: AI dispatch idle: brief dry, self-re-arm chain may have broken

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop selected no task and the brief carries no `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED:` marker. Under the self-re-arm protocol the brief should never run dry on its own, so this usually means the last dispatched task did not append its next task (a broken chain), or all planned work is genuinely complete.

Review `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and either append the next task to re-arm the loop or unarm it deliberately, then close this issue.

title: Audit Ctrl+Shift+Delete CAD delete shortcut boundary

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Audit task 168's Ctrl+Shift+Delete binding source/docs-read-only; MAY edit only `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, and `change.md`.
Confirm `edit.delete_current_cad_cuboid` binds `Shortcut::new(Modifiers::CTRL | Modifiers::SHIFT, Key::Delete)`, resolves only to `Command::DeleteCurrentCadCuboid`, and uses the generic accelerator route plus exact-tracked-CAD guard.
Confirm conflicts stay empty, accelerator table count is 19, bare Delete/Cut/wrapper-world delete stay unchanged, and editor-actions/CAD/editor-state/Cargo/egui-host production projection/rendering are unchanged.
Run the task 169 verification gates from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; make no Rust source/test/automation edits.
MUST NOT append task 170 except as the final-step next bounded FEATURE task if it is in-policy; otherwise record exactly one `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for human approval" block.
The task records whether a next bounded FEATURE task is in-policy; if not, exactly one `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for human approval" block is recorded.
No task 170 is appended unless it is the final bounded FEATURE task permitted by this task's final step.

title: AI dispatch idle: brief dry, self-re-arm chain may have broken

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop selected no task and the brief carries no `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED:` marker. Under the self-re-arm protocol the brief should never run dry on its own, so this usually means the last dispatched task did not append its next task (a broken chain), or all planned work is genuinely complete.

Review `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and either append the next task to re-arm the loop or unarm it deliberately, then close this issue.

Share the exact tracked-CAD selection rule between menu enablement and routing; do not modify `commands.rs`, editor-actions, CAD crates, Cargo files, Delete/Cut behavior, or egui-host rendering/projection logic.
Done when the menu and command palette expose "Delete Current CAD Cuboid", enabled only for the exact single tracked CAD selection, with warn-and-swallow rejection and all listed verification passing.

**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded
source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 167 - an audit of the menu-affordance CAD
delete boundary (the new `Command::DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` routes ONLY to
`delete_current_cad_cuboid`; its menu enablement and the route guard share the ONE
exact-tracked-CAD rule; rejection is warn-and-swallow with no fallback/stack
growth; the existing `Command::Delete`, Delete-key accelerator, Cut, and
wrapper-world delete are unchanged; `editor-actions` stays generic; the

unchanged; editor-egui-host rendering/projection logic is unchanged with only its
`menu_tests.rs` / `shortcut_help.rs` fixtures updated for the new entry). Task 167
is a GATED audit: it MUST
NOT append task 168 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for
human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks,
verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction verbatim into the
task 167 block it authors. Task 166 itself MUST NOT directly record a
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` marker UNLESS it cannot safely append the task 167 audit -
appending task 167 is the required primary outcome. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
to do this.

body:
Add one explicit Edit-menu command `DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` routed to the audited headless `EditorShell::delete_current_cad_cuboid` path.
In scope: `crates/editor-ui/src/menus/{command.rs,predicate.rs,default_menu.rs}`, editor-ui tests, `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/{mod.rs,tests.rs}`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts only.
Done: menu and command palette show "Delete Current CAD Cuboid" with no accelerator; enabled exactly for the sole tracked-CAD selection; routing warn-and-swallows rejected CAD delete with no fallback or stack growth; existing Delete/Cut/wrapper-world behavior, editor-actions, editor-egui-host, frozen CAD action, Cargo, docs, scripts, and workflows stay unchanged.
Verification: run the listed editor-ui/editor-shell tests, `cargo check -p rge-editor-ui -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor-egui-host`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, no-CAD-coupling rg over editor-actions, path-scoped diff checks, and task-heading rg.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 167 - an audit of the menu-affordance CAD delete boundary (the new `Command::DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` routes ONLY to `delete_current_cad_cuboid`; its menu enablement and the route guard share the ONE exact-tracked-CAD rule; rejection is warn-and-swallow with no fallback/stack growth; the existing `Command::Delete`, Delete-key accelerator, Cut, and wrapper-world delete are unchanged; `editor-actions` stays generic; the `DeleteCurrentCadCuboid` action + `delete_current_cad_cuboid` entry point are unchanged; `editor-egui-host` is unchanged). Task 167 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task 168 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks, verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction verbatim into the task 167 block it authors. Task 166 itself MUST NOT directly record a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` marker UNLESS it cannot safely append the task 167 audit - appending task 167 is the required primary outcome. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, and generated current-dispatch artifacts; do not edit `editor-actions`, CAD crates, UI/host crates, Cargo, workflows, schemas, or automation.

body:
Widen `editor-actions` with a minimal generic action-context contract while preserving object-safe `Box<dyn Action>`, existing World-only behavior, coalescing, ledger, dirty/save-mark, and no CAD/editor-shell deps.
In `editor-shell`, define the CAD action/context and route `EditorShell::add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` through `command_bus.submit`; keep scope to editor-actions + editor-shell lifecycle tests, with no cad-core/cad-projection/render/UI/Cargo changes.
Done means bus-routed cuboid add, undo, and redo work; revert restores pre-add graph/projection/world state with no stale entity/mesh, existing empty-scene rejection and stale-id normalization still hold, and apply-time undo metadata is handled without unsafe/global state.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):**
After implementation and verification, APPEND exactly one bounded
source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 161 - an audit of the post-bus-routed
first-CAD-mutation boundary. Task 161 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task
162 or any feature task, and its final step MUST record the next
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` marker plus a "Recommendation for
human approval" block (proposed next feature, exact edit surface, risks,
verification, why smallest). Carry this gated-audit instruction into the task
161 block it authors. Task 160 itself MUST NOT directly record a
`NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED` marker UNLESS it cannot safely append the task 161 audit
- appending task 161 is the required primary outcome. Edit
`.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this.

Call `EditorShell::clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` inside `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` before the existing `cad_entity` empty-scene guard, so stale tracked-id-only residue can be normalized without widening the first-cuboid boundary.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; do not edit CAD core/projection, CommandBus/actions, render path, UI/host, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Done: stale-id-only shells can add the first cuboid; live tracked CAD entities and any installed graph/projection/world/render/uploaded state still reject as `SceneNotEmpty`; cleanup remains liveness-only and clears only `self.cad_entity`.
Self-re-arm (final step, required) - BRIEF-ENFORCED PAUSE (operator decision):
After implementation and verification, append exactly one bounded source/docs-read-only AUDIT task as task 159 - an audit of the post-stale-id preflight-normalization boundary. Task 159 is a GATED audit: it MUST NOT append task 160 or any feature/implementation task, and MUST NOT self-re-arm with a follow-up feature. title: AI dispatch NEEDS_HUMAN: stale tracked-CAD cleanup caller approval

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop recorded a NEEDS_HUMAN boundary in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` after task 157 and cannot self-re-arm without a human decision.

Recorded marker:
NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: 2026-06-16 - stale tracked-CAD cleanup helper is implemented and tested, but approving its first production caller needs a human decision

Recommendation for human approval:
- Proposed next feature: wire `EditorShell::clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` into the existing `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene` empty-scene preflight so a stale stored `cad_entity` alone can be cleared before the current first-cuboid add guard runs, while preserving the existing rejection for installed CAD graph/projection/world state, render-only content, and other partial/non-empty scenes.
- Exact edit surface: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`; use `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` only if an adjacent lifecycle export or route adjustment is strictly required; generated current-dispatch handoff/audit artifacts only.
- Risks: the helper currently has no production caller; routing it through UI, CommandBus, dirty/save, graph restore, projection despawn, render refresh, mesh upload, or GPU paths would expand authority beyond the cleanup primitive. The approved feature should only normalize stale tracked-id state before the existing add preflight and should not turn render-mesh absence into a cleanup predicate.
- Verification: run focused `clear_stale_tracked_cad_entity` and `cad_scene_inspection` lifecycle tests, add a route-specific lifecycle test for stale-id-only preflight normalization, run `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, the lifecycle/projection evidence greps from this audit, `git diff --name-only`, and `git diff --check`.
- Why this is the smallest coherent next step: current source already has a narrow stale-id cleanup primitive and tests, and `rg` shows it is not wired to a production caller. The proposed follow-up decides one caller boundary without changing graph/projection cleanup policy, render behavior, CommandBus/undo/dirty/save authority, UI routing, CAD-core, or cad-projection.

Local gate state:
- `origin/main` contains ISSUE-412 commit `5f39c96`.
- `.ai/dispatch.auto-halt` is present and pauses subsequent Auto ticks.
- No task 158 heading was appended.

This issue was filed manually after verification because the local halt sentinel claimed a needs-human issue had been filed, but GitHub had no open `needs-human` issue for this boundary.

title: Clear stale tracked-CAD entity ids

body:

Return `true` only when a previously stored stale id is cleared; return `false` for fresh/no tracked id/live tracked id; do not despawn, tick projection, restore/rollback graph state, mutate selection/camera, enqueue actions, mark dirty/saved, save/load/open/close, initialize render state, upload meshes, touch GPU, or edit forbidden surfaces.
Tests must cover fresh shell no-op, successful first cuboid add no-op, `restore_to(pre_add_head)` plus `despawn_brep_entity` stale cleanup where inspection reports present true/live false before helper and present false after, while `cad_scene_inspection` remains read-only and consistent.

Add a read-only `EditorShell::cad_scene_inspection()`-style API and snapshot type for CAD graph/projection/renderability after `add_cad_cuboid_to_empty_scene`; keep it headless and read-only.
MAY edit `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/{commands.rs,mod.rs,tests.rs}` and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; MUST NOT touch `render_path.rs`, `editor-actions`, `cad-core`, `cad-projection`, UI/host crates, Cargo, workflows, automation, render/GPU behavior, CommandBus, save/load, camera, or viewport behavior.
Done: snapshot distinguishes fresh shell, successful first cuboid add, rejected non-empty add, and restore/despawn cleanup; tests assert one CAD node/root, one `BRepHandle`, one `render_mesh_for` mesh, no prebuilt substitution, and no stale entity/mesh after cleanup.
Execution note: uploaded mesh count is a PURE read of `EditorShell.meshes` / `self.meshes.len()`; do NOT initialize render state, upload meshes, resume the window, or touch `render_path.rs`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 153 - a source/docs-read-only audit of the post-inspection CAD/editor-shell feature boundary - or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a single `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. body:
Add a bounded shell entry point that inserts exactly one CuboidOp into an empty/new CAD scene using existing public APIs: begin_operation -> add_operator -> set_root -> commit -> projection tick/spawn if needed.
MAY edit editor-shell lifecycle commands/mod/tests plus render_path only for a minimal existing-path refresh hook; MUST NOT edit editor-actions/CommandBus, cad-core, cad-projection, UI/host, Cargo, workflows, or automation.

title: AI dispatch NEEDS_HUMAN: CAD/CommandBus first-mutation recommendation (task 149)

labels.name: needs-human
labels.description: AI dispatch paused for human review/decision

body:
The autonomous dispatch loop's task 149 (ISSUE-403, read-only CAD/CommandBus preflight) recorded a NEEDS_HUMAN recommendation in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and paused. Per the gate, it did NOT append an implementation task. Approve, redirect, or hold before any CAD mutation code is authored.

**Recommendation (Codex preflight):**
- **Proposed mutation:** a bus-routed "add one CAD cuboid primitive" command as the first real CAD mutation — submit one reversible CAD action through `CommandBus` after approving a narrow editor-owned command context (the current `Action::apply/revert` contract only receives `&mut World`, while live CAD state lives in `EditorShell` as `cad_world`/`cad_graph`/`projection`/`cad_entity`).
- **Edit surface:** `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs`, `bus.rs` (+ action tests); `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`, `mod.rs`; `render_path.rs`; focused tests. Reuse existing `CadGraph::begin_operation/graph_mut/commit`, `OperatorGraph::add_operator/set_root`, `CadProjection::spawn_brep_entity/tick`.
- **Risks:** widening the bus action context beyond World-only; keeping graph commit, projection tick/cache invalidation, render-mesh upload, undo/revert, dirty-state, save-mark, and save/load coherent on success AND failed apply/revert.
- **Verification:** action unit coverage for the widened context + bus undo/redo/dirty/save-mark; lifecycle tests proving add-cuboid submit/undo/redo round-trips graph/projection/world/render; save/load dirty tests; render/projection tests showing the primitive is frameable/selectable.
- **Why smallest:** adding one primitive needs only create/commit/project/display/undo/dirty/save-mark — fewer concerns than delete, transform, edit-params, clear/replace, or doc-lifecycle alternatives.

Filed manually after the driver's auto-filing failed twice; see notification follow-up.

Implement task 147: `EditorShell::zoom_camera_in` and `zoom_camera_out` clear pending `ViewportLeftDoubleClick` before existing `zoom_camera_by`; preserve zoom math, routing, valid double-click framing, reset-camera/wheel/focus/cursor/orbit/pan behavior.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, and generated ISSUE artifacts.
Done: tests cover first viewport left press + View/PageUp Zoom In and View/PageDown Zoom Out + second in-threshold left press not triggering stale scene or selected-CAD framing; run the listed cargo/fmt/diff gates.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append
exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 148 - a "Post-zoom-command-double-click-reset Phase 9 next-task source audit"
(docs/source-read-only) - or, if no bounded in-policy next task exists, append a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. Mirror
task 146's audit structure (sections, scope discipline) but not its task-number-specific criteria: task 148 is itself a self-re-arming audit, so
its done-criteria must require appending exactly one bounded FEATURE task 149 or recording `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED`.

title: Reset stale viewport double-click on reset camera

body:
Implement `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task 145 exactly: when `EditorShell::reset_camera` runs via View -> Reset Camera / Frame Scene or Home, clear pending `ViewportLeftDoubleClick` state before a later in-threshold left press can complete a stale double-click.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, and generated dispatch artifacts; preserve all task-145 MUST-NOT surfaces.
Done: focused tests prove first viewport left press + View/Home reset-camera + second in-threshold left press does not frame stale scene/selected bounds, while existing reset-camera and viewport double-click behavior remain unchanged.
Verification: `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib reset_camera`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_left_double_click`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_mouse_wheel`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_focus_loss`; `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib viewport_cursor_left`; `rg -n "^143\.|^144\.|^145\.|^146\." .ai/dispatch.tasks.md`; `git diff --name-only`; `git diff --check`.
**Self-re-arm (final step, required):** After the implementation is complete
   and verified, follow the Self-re-arm protocol in this file's header: append
   exactly one bounded next-task-source AUDIT task as task 146 - a

   append a `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED: <ISO-date> - <reason>` line instead. Mirror

   carrying the copied Self-re-arm requirement, or a single
   `NEEDS_HUMAN_RECORDED:` line. Copy this Self-rearm final-step requirement
   into the audit task you author. Edit `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to do this
   (already in `MAY edit`).

body:
Implement task 141: reset pending `ViewportLeftDoubleClick` state on `WindowEvent::Focused(false)` / `handle_window_focus_change(false)`, while preserving existing focus-loss drag cancellation/cursor-grab release and focus-gained no-op behavior.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, allowed status/task docs, and generated dispatch artifacts only.
Add focused lifecycle coverage for first left press -> focus loss -> second in-threshold left press not framing scene or selected CAD bounds; run the listed focused editor-shell tests plus `git diff --name-only` and `git diff --check`.

title: Frame selected CAD entities on viewport double-click

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 139: on qualifying viewport left double-click, frame selected CAD projection entity bounds first, falling back to existing scene-wide `reset_camera()`.
In scope: `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, status docs/task bookkeeping, and this dispatch's artifacts.
Use only existing `EditorShell` state plus read-only render-mesh/AABB helpers such as `CadProjection::render_mesh_for`, `compute_aabb_union`, and `isometric_camera_for_bounds`.
Preserve the existing double-click detector, single-click face-pick path, View camera commands, wheel zoom, orbit, pan, viewport hit testing, and command routing.
Do not edit CAD/projection crates, editor-ui, editor-egui-host, editor-actions, Cargo files, workflows, automation, or add commands/accelerators.
Done: selected single/multiple CAD bounds frame correctly; no selection/unresolved/non-finite/prebuilt path falls back to frame-all; tests cover both plus unchanged face-pick behavior.

---

title: Cancel viewport drags when cursor leaves

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement cursor-leave handling in `editor-shell`: clear `cursor_pos`, reset the viewport double-click tracker, cancel active orbit/pan drags, and release `CursorGrabMode::None` only when a drag was active.
MAY edit only `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, status/task docs, and generated ISSUE artifacts.
MUST NOT change camera math, viewport hit testing, picking, wheel zoom, frame-all, command routing, shortcuts, plugin/runtime, OS clipboard, CAD/CommandBus, save/load, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Add focused tests for active orbit cancel, active pan cancel, combined drag release-once, no-active no-release, cursor-position clearing, and stale double-click reset.
Verify with the taskâ€™s focused `rge-editor-shell` tests, `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`, `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`, task-heading grep, `git diff --name-only`, and `git diff --check`.

---

title: Release viewport drag cursor grab on focus loss

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 135 from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`: cancel active viewport orbit/pan drag state on `WindowEvent::Focused(false)` and release the existing cursor grab only when a viewport drag was active.
Scope is `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`, and status/task docs.
Preserve current camera math, viewport hit testing, wheel zoom, face picking, frame-all, command routing, shortcuts, plugin, clipboard, CAD, undo/dirty, and save/load behavior.
Done when focused tests cover active orbit/pan focus-loss cancellation plus the no-active-drag no-release case, and the listed editor-shell checks/fmt/diff gates pass.

---

title: Make conflicted shortcuts non-executable

labels.name: ai-auto
labels.description: Task selected by the autonomous dispatch driver

body:
Implement task 123: keyboard execution must not fire a shortcut while `ResolveResult::conflicts` contains that shortcut.
Scope to editor-ui menu/shortcut resolution and focused tests, with only minimal editor-shell accelerator parity/comments if needed plus status/task docs.
Keep `command_for_shortcut` / `AcceleratorTable::resolve` first-winner behavior for display and diagnostics.
Preserve hidden/predicate-filtered entries releasing slots and disabled-visible entries remaining non-executable.
Do not change `MenuCommandHandoff`, `route_menu_command`, plugin runtime, OS/typed clipboard, CAD/CommandBus, camera, Cargo, workflows, or automation.
Done when tests cover conflicted suppression, unconflicted execution, disabled suppression, predicate filtering, diagnostics order, and no task 124 is appended.

title: Reconcile stale Editor-usability preflight findings in plans/BASELINE.md (post-Phase-9 keyboard wiring)
body:
# Task Packet

DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-256
AUTHOR: Planner / Anthropic Claude
TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-29_01-59-05+0300
RELATED_FILES:
- plans/BASELINE.md
- Status.md
- HANDOFF.md
- change.md
- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-254_EXEC_2026-05-28_23-49-37+0300.md
- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs
- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs
- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs
- crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs
- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
- crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs
- crates/editor-ui/src/dock/spawner_registry.rs
- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-256_*
- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-256*.md
STATUS: OPEN

## Goal

Reconcile the now-substantially-stale Phase 9 Editor-usability preflight
section in `plans/BASELINE.md` (`:541-639`, specifically the current-state
claims spanning `:586-620`) via a forward-only prepended subsection,
grounded ENTIRELY in the correction-loop-verified audit
`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-254_EXEC_2026-05-28_23-49-37+0300.md` and re-confirmable
against current source at main `6e24706`.

This dispatch supersedes the abandoned ISSUE-253 (control-blocked +
closed not-planned), which asserted "Gap 2 UNCHANGED" and "neither
revisit trigger fired" by extrapolating from the dated 2026-05-21 text
rather than reading current source. The #254 audit was filed expressly
to ground-truth the current state before this reconciliation. The audit's
findings (which this dispatch records) are:

- **Gap 1** (`:588`, scene/project persistence)  PARTIALLY STALE. Load
  direction is wired (`--scene` via ISSUE-225; `--glb` via dispatch G);
  SAVE direction has no path at all.
- **Gap 2** (`:590`, Command Bus unreachable from editor UI) 
  SUBSTANTIALLY CLOSED. Six Ctrl-bound commands + two plain-key playback
  commands now route from `window_event` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:1676`)
  through the bus / PlayState. The narrower limitation that the bus
  Action context stays World-only is STILL CURRENT (a different, smaller
  gap).
- **Gap 3** (`:592`, MenuRegistry + io-* loaders)  PARTIALLY STALE.
  `io-gltf::import_glb` IS called via `--glb`; MenuRegistry +
  `io-image` + drag-drop remain absent.
- **Revisit triggers** (`:615-620`): Trigger 2 (non-CAD user-input path)
  FIRED via Space/Escape PIE keybindings; Trigger 1 (CommandBus
  integration design decision) is AMBIGUOUS  implementation landed, no
  formal design artifact exists. The deferral guidance at `:620` is no
  longer in force.

The reconciliation must also call out a PHANTOM REFERENCE the audit
surfaced: BASELINE.md's narrative (and ISSUE-251's 2026-05-28
live-inspector reconciliation) refers in passing to a "CommandBus
integration design preflight (decided the bus stays World-only)"  but
NO such standalone section exists; the reference is self-referential
drift. This dispatch records the World-only state as IMPLICIT-VIA-SHIPPED-CODE
(grounding line `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:87`, unchanged), NOT
as a prior formal decision. It MUST NOT retroactively author an ADR or
"design preflight" artifact to validate that phantom reference (that
anti-pattern is precisely what is being avoided).

Without this reconciliation, the next Phase 9 audit re-discovers the
2026-05-21 "defer all user-facing editor wire-up dispatches" /
"structurally unreachable from the editor UI" / "no loop to
friction-test" claims as current, wasting a cycle  and the phantom
"design preflight" reference stays uncorrected.

## Scope

### MAY edit

- `plans/BASELINE.md`  prepend a new dated subsection
  `### 2026-05-28  Editor-usability preflight reconciliation
  (post-ISSUE-225 / dispatch-G / ISSUE-249 + Phase-9 keyboard wiring)`
  IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the existing `### 2026-05-21  initial
  editor-usability adoption snapshot` subsection at `:561`. The entire
  2026-05-21 dated content (`:561` through the end of the
  Editor-usability section at `:639`) MUST be preserved byte-identical;
  reconciliation is by addition above `:561`, never by in-place edit.
  The section's framing paragraphs ABOVE `:561` (`## Editor-usability
  preflight (Phase 9)` heading, Budget anchors `:543-549`, "This entry
  is a Phase 9 PREFLIGHT" paragraph, Methodology) are also preserved
  byte-identical  the prepend goes between the Methodology block and
  the `### 2026-05-21` subsection header.
- `Status.md`  prepend a forward-only snapshot above the existing
  most-recent snapshot (ISSUE-251's 2026-05-28 live-inspector
  reconciliation snapshot), matching the ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245 /
  ISSUE-251 precedent.
- `HANDOFF.md`  parallel snapshot prepended above the existing
  most-recent snapshot.
- `change.md`  insert a 2026-05-28 entry after the most-recent existing
  2026-05-28 entry (ISSUE-251's) and before the 2026-05-23 15:20 REPO
  TRANSFER entry, matching the ISSUE-245 / ISSUE-251 insertion pattern.

### MAY add new files

- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-256_*.md`
- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-256_*.meta.json`
- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-256*.md`

### MUST NOT edit

- Any Rust source, test, bench, or fixture under `crates/**`,
  `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, `tools/**`, or `editor/**`. (All source
  files in RELATED_FILES are read-only grounding references, NOT edit
  targets.)
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, or any `**/Cargo.toml`.
- Any other file under `plans/**`  `plans/PLAN.md`,
  `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `plans/fileandfolderstructure.md`,
  `plans/README.md`.
- Any ADR under `docs/adr/**`. (This dispatch does NOT author an ADR 
  see the phantom-reference / anti-pattern note in Goal and the explicit
  Halt Condition.)
- Any other subsection of `plans/BASELINE.md` (W03 / W04 / W08 / W10 /
  6.3 / 13.2 / Live-inspector wiring preflight / and the
  Editor-usability section's own `:622-639` Notes/caveats block) 
  byte-identical to pre-edit.
- The 2026-05-21 "initial editor-usability adoption snapshot" dated
  content (`:561-639`)  preserved verbatim, including the Status line
  (`:609`), Revisit triggers (`:615-620`), and Notes/caveats
  (`:622-639`). Even where the new prepended subsection notes that some
  of this content aged (e.g. `:625` rejected-micro-dispatch (b) shipped
  as `--glb`), the dated text is NOT edited in place.
- Architecture-lint source, fixtures, snapshots, exemptions.
- Any workflow under `.github/workflows/**`.
- `Invoke-AiDispatch*.ps1`, `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`,
  `Get-AiDispatch*.ps1`, `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`, `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`,
  `new-handoff.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`,
  `.ai/*.schema.json`.
- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`, `ai_handoffs/templates/**`, any
  prior `ai_handoffs/*` packet or sidecar (including the ISSUE-254 audit
  packet  read-only grounding), any prior `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`.

### MUST NOT add new files

- No new Rust source / test / bench / fixture / crate / Cargo manifest.
- No new ADR, architecture-lint file, automation script, schema, or
  workflow file.
- No new doc file under `plans/`, `docs/`, or repo root.
- No new `.github/` files.

## Deliverables

- New `### 2026-05-28  Editor-usability preflight reconciliation
  (post-ISSUE-225 / dispatch-G / ISSUE-249 + Phase-9 keyboard wiring)`
  subsection prepended above the 2026-05-21 subsection. It MUST:
  - State the headline current reality: load paths exist (`--glb`,
    `--scene`); `--scene` renders a visible window (ISSUE-249); six
    Ctrl-bound + two plain-key keyboard commands route through the bus /
    PlayState; SAVE direction absent; bus Action context still
    World-only; MenuRegistry / io-image / drag-drop absent.
  - Classify each gap with the audit's verdicts AND current-source
    citations: Gap 1 PARTIALLY STALE (load wired / save absent); Gap 2
    SUBSTANTIALLY CLOSED (keyboardbus wired; World-only context is the
    residual narrower gap); Gap 3 PARTIALLY STALE (io-gltf wired;
    MenuRegistry/io-image/drag-drop absent).
  - Record the revisit-trigger reality precisely: Trigger 2 FIRED
    (Space/Escape PIE keybindings at `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs`);
    Trigger 1 AMBIGUOUS  "implementation landed, formal design artifact
    absent." State explicitly that the World-only CommandBus posture is
    IMPLICIT-VIA-SHIPPED-CODE (`crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:87`,
    unchanged), NOT a documented prior decision, and that the
    "defer all user-facing editor wire-up dispatches" guidance at `:620`
    is no longer in force.
  - Call out the phantom reference: the "CommandBus integration design
    preflight (decided the bus stays World-only)" cross-reference (in
    the live-inspector narrative + ISSUE-251's reconciliation) points to
    a section that does not exist; the World-only state is implicit, not
    formally decided. Mark this as governance-surface drift to be
    corrected by acknowledgment, explicitly NOT by authoring the
    missing artifact.
  - Note in passing (without in-place edit) that the dated `:625`
    rejected-micro-dispatch (b) ("`--load <gltf-path>` invokes
    `io-gltf::import_glb`") was subsequently shipped as `--glb`, as part
    of explaining how the section aged.
  - Enumerate the still-open gaps the audit grounded (audit 4 Gaps
    4-10): save-direction absent; menu-command execution absent;
    drag-drop absent; io-image unused; non-CLI open/load UX absent;
    bus coverage is keyboard-shaped not CAD-shaped (World-only Action
    context); spawner registry still registers `PlaceholderTabBody` for
    `"tab/inspector"` (inspector renders via the host-internal
    `TabBody::Inspector`, a separate path).
  - PRESERVE explicitly (name them so the future audit sees the
    reconciliation is bounded): the `:573-582` "Workflows that work
    end-to-end TODAY" table, the `:584` test-coverage paragraph, and the
    entire `:622-639` Notes/caveats block as dated methodology history.
  - Cite closure evidence with file/line refs at main `6e24706`:
    `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` (EditorKeyCommand +
    `command_bus.submit` at `:280`); `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs:110-123`
    + `:156-188` (Space  TogglePlay / Escape  Stop);
    `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1676` (KeyboardInput branch;
    catch-all moved to `:1725`); `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:279-285`
    (post-ISSUE-249 init split  `has_cad_scene || has_prebuilt_mesh`
    guards Phase 2 only), `:313-328` (EguiHost construction +
    InspectorHandoff stash), `:510-610` (the egui-only `render_frame`
    branch that makes the `--scene` window visible);
    `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:912-968` (`--scene`), `:612`
    (`import_glb`), `:971-1026` (`--glb`);
    `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml:36-38` (`--scene` rge-data /
    rge-scene-loader / ron deps), `:29` (rge-io-gltf dep);
    `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:87` (World-only Action
    signature, unchanged); `crates/editor-ui/src/dock/spawner_registry.rs:165-168`
    (`PlaceholderTabBody` unchanged); audit packet
    `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-254_EXEC_2026-05-28_23-49-37+0300.md`.
- Forward-only snapshots in `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`,
  matching the ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245 / ISSUE-251 forward-only snapshot
  precedent. Older dated content preserved verbatim.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `plans/BASELINE.md` diff: pure prepend immediately above
  `### 2026-05-21  initial editor-usability adoption snapshot`. The
  2026-05-21 dated content (`:561-639` pre-edit) is byte-identical
  post-edit. The Editor-usability section's framing above `:561` is
  byte-identical. Verify with `git diff -- plans/BASELINE.md` showing
  only additions in that one location.
- Every other subsection of `plans/BASELINE.md` (W03 / W04 / W08 / W10 /
  6.3 / 13.2 / Live-inspector wiring preflight) AND the
  Editor-usability `:622-639` Notes/caveats block are byte-identical.
- **Anti-#253 grounding criteria  the new subsection MUST NOT make any
  of the following claims (each was a #253-class premise error or its
  symmetric inverse); where the topic is addressed, current source MUST
  be cited:**
  1. NOT "Gap 2 is UNCHANGED"  it is SUBSTANTIALLY CLOSED; cite
     `commands.rs:280` + `mod.rs:1676`.
  2. NOT "`lifecycle.rs:776` catch-all swallows every KeyboardInput" 
     the catch-all moved to `mod.rs:1725`, downstream of a real
     KeyboardInput arm at `mod.rs:1676`.
  3. NOT "zero CommandBus call sites in editor-shell / rge-editor" 
     cite `commands.rs:280` (+ undo/redo/mark_saved sites).
  4. NOT "no `--scene` / `.rge-project` ingestion"  cite
     `main.rs:912-968` + `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml:36-38`.
  5. NOT "`io-gltf::import_glb` has zero call sites in editor surface" 
     cite `main.rs:612` (+ `--glb` at `:971-1026`,
     `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml:29`).
  6. NOT "`io-image` is USED by the editor" (the symmetric inverse
     error)  it is NOT; cite the zero-match grep. The correct claim is
     "zero io-image call sites in the editor surface."
  7. NOT "the bus grew a CAD context"  `action.rs:87` is World-only,
     unchanged.
  8. NOT "a CommandBus integration design ADR exists"  none exists;
     the decision is implicit-via-code. (And do NOT author one.)
  9. NOT "MenuRegistry is reached from the editor surface"  it is NOT;
     distinguish the `play_toolbar.rs:12` doc-comment cross-reference
     from a functional construction/query.
  10. NOT "the `tab/inspector` spawner was upgraded"  it still
      registers `PlaceholderTabBody` (`spawner_registry.rs:165-168`);
      the inspector renders via the host-internal `TabBody::Inspector`.
- The new subsection records Trigger 2 FIRED, Trigger 1 AMBIGUOUS
  (implementation-landed-formal-record-absent), and the phantom-reference
  drift callout, WITHOUT authoring any artifact to resolve the phantom.
- `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md` forward-only; older dated
  content byte-identical; `change.md` entry at the ISSUE-251-pattern
  location.
- The EXEC's `change.md` cargo-gate paragraph uses the corrected
  ISSUE-251 wording: "NO standalone cargo commands outside
  `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`; NO `cargo bench`, no standalone `cargo
  test`, no fresh interactive editor run." (NOT "NO Cargo command run".)
- No edit outside the MAY-edit envelope.
- `git diff --check`  exit 0.
- `git status --short --branch` final  tracked changes confined to the
  MAY-edit envelope.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`  exit 0.

## Constraints / Non-Goals

- This dispatch does NOT author an ADR, design note, or "CommandBus
  integration design preflight" section. The phantom reference is
  reconciled by ACKNOWLEDGMENT (noting it never existed; the state is
  implicit-via-code), NOT by manufacturing the referenced artifact. If
  a forward-looking CommandBus decision record is later desired, it is
  a SEPARATE present-dated chip, out of scope here.
- This dispatch does NOT expand beyond the Editor-usability section. The
  `:622-639` Notes/caveats block stays byte-identical (load-bearing
  methodology record; the new subsection may reference its aging items
  in prose but does not edit them).
- This dispatch does NOT reconcile other plans docs, the Live-inspector
  section (already reconciled by ISSUE-251), or any dated baseline row.
- This dispatch does NOT declare Trigger 1 "fired." It records the
  asymmetric reality (implementation landed / formal record absent). A
  determination that Trigger 1 fully fired is a human-arbitration call,
  not a docs-chip call.
- This dispatch does NOT add measurement / benchmark / test results, new
  thresholds, or new revisit triggers.
- This dispatch does NOT change the publication path: PR-mode queue
  publication; no direct `origin/main` push; no auto-close of the issue.

## Verification Gates

The Executor MUST run and document each in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:

- `git status --short --branch` before any edit  expected clean on the
  dispatch branch.
- Re-read the audit packet
  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-254_EXEC_2026-05-28_23-49-37+0300.md` 2-5 AND
  re-confirm each closure-evidence citation against current source at
  main `6e24706` (the new subsection's claims must be re-grounded, not
  copied from the audit on faith  the audit is the map, current source
  is the territory).
- `git diff -- plans/BASELINE.md` after edit  expected: pure additions
  immediately above `### 2026-05-21  initial editor-usability adoption
  snapshot`; ZERO changes to `:561-639` (pre-edit numbering) and ZERO
  changes to any other subsection.
- `git diff -- Status.md HANDOFF.md change.md`  expected: pure prepend
  (Status, HANDOFF) or single inserted entry (change at the
  ISSUE-251-pattern location); older dated content byte-identical.
- `git diff --check`  exit 0.
- `git status --short --branch` final  tracked changes confined to the
  four MAY-edit files plus this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-256_*`
  and `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-256*.md`.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`  exit 0.

No standalone cargo gates are required beyond `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
That canonical gate mirrors CI and may run fmt, architecture lints, and
workspace tests. Do not run additional cargo commands outside that gate
unless a specific reason is documented.

## Halt Conditions

The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`,
`NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 2` if any of the following occur:

- Reconciling would require any in-place edit to the 2026-05-21 dated
  content (`:561-639`) rather than pure prepending above `:561`.
- Reconciling would require touching any other `plans/BASELINE.md`
  subsection or the `:622-639` Notes/caveats block.
- A current-source re-confirmation CONTRADICTS the audit's classification
  (i.e. source moved between the audit at `273d658`/`6e24706` and this
  dispatch such that a gap verdict no longer holds). Halt rather than
  publish a verdict that fails re-grounding  this is the #253 failure
  mode and the explicit reason for the re-confirm gate.
- The reconciliation appears to require authoring an ADR / design note /
  "design preflight" section to make Trigger 1 or the phantom reference
  resolve cleanly. Authoring that artifact is the anti-pattern this
  dispatch exists to avoid; surface it for human arbitration instead.
- More than one follow-up dispatch would be required to make the
  reconciliation honest.
- Tracked files are dirty before any edit is attempted.

## Planner Notes

This dispatch is grounded in the correction-loop-verified audit
`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-254_EXEC_2026-05-28_23-49-37+0300.md` (passed Codex
control after 2 CORRECT rounds), which was filed expressly to ground-truth
the current state after ISSUE-253 control-blocked on a stale premise. The
audit's 5 "Recommended Next Reconciliation Scope" defines this chip's
scope (`:586-620` cohesive prepend), and its 5 "Exact claims the future
reconciliation TASK must NOT make without fresh source reads" is encoded
verbatim as the 10-item Anti-#253 grounding criteria above.

Directly-mirrored precedent: ISSUE-251's `plans/BASELINE.md`
live-inspector reconciliation (merged `273d658`)  same forward-only
prepend pattern, change.md insertion location, dispatch.verify.ps1
gate wording, and byte-identical-preservation discipline. ISSUE-249
(merged `007635d`, `--scene` window) and dispatch-G (`--glb`) are
closure evidence, NOT snapshot precedents.

Intended publication path: queue PR mode
(`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -PublishMode pr`).

Per orchestrator dispatch semantics in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`:
TASK + EXEC packets only, plus optional CORRECT. Missing `_REVIEW_` /
`_CLOSEOUT_` packets is normal  the Codex control review goes to the
gitignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-256/codex.control.round0.json`.

---

HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-256
AUTHOR: Planner / Anthropic Claude
NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
EXIT_CODE: 0

but current source already contains CommandBus keyboard integration,
Ctrl-bound editor commands, and playback keyboard bindings. The next

## Scope

### MAY add new files

### MUST NOT edit

- Any Rust source, test, bench, or fixture under crates/**,

### MUST NOT add new files

- No new Rust source / test / bench / fixture / crate / Cargo manifest.
- No new ADR, architecture-lint file, automation script, schema, workflow
  file, plan doc, or root doc.
- No new file outside the ISSUE-254 handoff/log globs listed under
  MAY add new files.

## Deliverables

Write an EXECUTION_REPORT that contains a clear, cited audit answer
block with these exact sections:

1. Current Source Evidence Read
   - List every file inspected and the exact current line ranges used as
     evidence. At minimum inspect:
     plans/BASELINE.md Editor-usability preflight section;
     editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs; editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml;
     crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs;
     crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs;
     crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs;
     crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs; and targeted searches for
     CommandBus, KeyboardInput, Ctrl+, Space, Escape,
     MenuRegistry, esolve, io_gltf, import_glb, io_image,
     load_path, load_bytes, drag, drop, open_file,
     load_project, and save_project.

2. Gap 1 / 2 / 3 Current Status
   - For each of the plans/BASELINE.md:586-592 Top 3 usability gaps,
     classify current status as one of CURRENT, PARTIALLY STALE,
     SUBSTANTIALLY CLOSED, or STALE, and explain the classification
     with source citations.
   - Do not assume that a negative claim is still true. Any still absent,
     unchanged, or 
ot wired statement must cite current source or a
     targeted search result.

3. Revisit Trigger Status
   - Evaluate the literal revisit triggers in the 2026-05-21
     Editor-usability preflight section, especially the CommandBus
     integration trigger and the non-CAD keyboard/user-input trigger.
   - For each trigger, say FIRED, NOT FIRED, or AMBIGUOUS, with
     evidence. If a trigger is ambiguous, explain exactly what human
     decision is needed before reconciliation.

4. Still-Open Usability Gaps
   - List the remaining current gaps that are actually supported by source
     evidence. Candidate areas to verify include save direction, menu
     command execution, drag-and-drop, io-image editor-surface usage,
     non-CLI open/load UX, and whether any CommandBus integration is only
     partial despite existing keyboard wiring.

5. Recommended Next Reconciliation Scope
   - Recommend the smallest safe follow-up docs reconciliation scope.
   - Explicitly answer whether a future chip should cover only
     plans/BASELINE.md:586-592, should expand through :594-620, or
     should cover a different/larger section boundary.
   - List any exact claims that a future reconciliation TASK must avoid
     unless grounded by fresh source reads.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The dispatch produces only ISSUE-254 handoff/log artifacts and no
  edits to existing tracked source/docs files.
- The EXECUTION_REPORT contains all five deliverable sections named
  above.
- Every positive and negative current-state claim in the audit has a
  citation to current source lines or a targeted search result.
- The audit explicitly calls out the ISSUE-253 control-block premise error:
  negative claims such as UNCHANGED, still absent, and 	rigger has not
  fired are not valid unless grounded against current source.
- The audit does not draft replacement prose for plans/BASELINE.md; it
  may recommend the next scope, but it must not perform the reconciliation.
- git diff --check exits 0.
- Final git status --short --branch shows no tracked edits, plus only this
  dispatch's own ^Gi_handoffs/ISSUE-254_* and
  ^Gi_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-254*.md artifacts.
- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1 exits 0.

## Constraints / Non-Goals

- This is an audit-only dispatch. It does not reconcile, edit, prepend, or
  rewrite plans/BASELINE.md, Status.md, HANDOFF.md, or change.md.
- This dispatch does not decide the next reconciliation wording. It gathers
  the evidence needed for a later, separately scoped docs chip.
- This dispatch does not add tests, run fresh editor samples, take new
  measurements, or introduce new thresholds.
- No standalone cargo gates are required beyond .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1.
  That canonical gate mirrors CI and may run fmt, architecture lints, and
  workspace tests. Do not run additional cargo commands outside that gate
  unless a specific reason is documented.
- This dispatch does not change publication policy: PR-mode queue
  publication; no direct origin/main push; no auto-close of the GitHub
  issue.

## Verification Gates

The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
EXECUTION_REPORT:

- git status --short --branch before any edit. Expected: clean on the
  dispatch branch except for dispatch-owned TASK artifacts if already
  scaffolded.
- Targeted source reads/searches sufficient to answer all five deliverable
  sections. Document commands and results.
- git diff --name-only. Expected: no existing tracked source/docs paths.
- git diff --check. Expected exit 0.
- git status --short --branch final. Expected: no tracked edits, plus only
  this dispatch's own ^Gi_handoffs/ISSUE-254_* and
  ^Gi_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-254*.md artifacts.
- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1. Expected exit 0.

## Halt Conditions

The Executor MUST halt with HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN,
NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER, EXIT_CODE: 2 if any of the following occur:

- Answering the audit would require editing any existing tracked file.
- The source evidence is internally contradictory enough that the five audit
  sections cannot be answered without a human decision.
- The executor cannot access the files needed to ground the negative claims.
- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1 exposes a failure that is not attributable to
  this audit-only dispatch.

## Planner Notes

ISSUE-253 is deliberately not being retried. It was closed as not planned
after Codex control found that its reconciliation premise was false. This
fresh audit issue is the safer audit-then-reconciliation reset.

The intended follow-up after this audit is a separate docs reconciliation
chip using the audit's evidence, not an automatic correction round against
ISSUE-253's stale premise.

Per the orchestrator dispatch semantics in AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md:
TASK + EXEC packets only, plus an optional CORRECT under a correction round.
Missing _REVIEW_ / _CLOSEOUT_ packets is normal - the Codex control
review goes to the gitignored .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-254/codex.control.round0.json,
not a packet.

title: Reconcile stale Editor-usability preflight findings in plans/BASELINE.md after ISSUE-225 and ISSUE-249

labels.name: ai-dispatch-failure-control

body:
# Task Packet  DRAFT

DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-253           # assigned at filing
AUTHOR: Planner / Anthropic Claude
TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-28_21-55-58+0300
RELATED_FILES:
- plans/BASELINE.md
- Status.md
- HANDOFF.md
- change.md
- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
- editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml
- crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs
- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-247_EXEC_2026-05-28_14-53-12+0300.md
- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-253_*
- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-253*.md
STATUS: OPEN

## Goal

Reconcile the now-partially-stale Phase 9 Editor-usability preflight
findings in `plans/BASELINE.md`'s "Top 3 usability gaps" block at
`:586-592` via a forward-only snapshot, mirroring the just-landed
ISSUE-251 reconciliation pattern. Three landed changes have overtaken
parts of those findings:

1. **`--glb <path>` CLI load path** (Dispatch G, predating this
   session's work) wires `rge-io-gltf` from the editor binary; closes
   "zero io-gltf call sites" + "no CLI argument parsing for a file
   path" sub-claims.
2. **`--scene <path>` CLI load path** (ISSUE-225, commit `598f27e`)
   parses `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene` RON via `rge-data` +
   `rge-scene-loader`, lands a fresh `Scene` into the editor's
   `World`. Closes "the editor never calls [RON serialization]"
   sub-claim for the load direction.
3. **`--scene` actually shows a window** (ISSUE-249, main `007635d`)
   `init_render_state` constructs window + surface + EguiHost +
   InspectorHandoff unconditionally; world-only `--scene` launches now
   render the egui dock + Inspector chrome. Closes the residual
   "hardcoded cuboid is the only shape that ever exists" framing for
   the load loop.

Gap 1 (`:588`) is therefore PARTIALLY STALE: load is wired; SAVE remains
absent. Gap 3 (`:592`) is PARTIALLY STALE: `io-gltf` + CLI-arg parsing
are wired; `io-image` + `MenuRegistry` + drag-drop still gaps. Gap 2
(`:590`, Command Bus from editor UI) is UNCHANGED.

Without this reconciliation, the next Phase 9 audit will rediscover
the 2026-05-21 snapshot's headline claims ("the hardcoded
`CuboidOp(1.0, 1.0, 1.0)` is the only shape that ever exists" / "Zero
string-literal matches for `.rge` / `.rgeproj` / `.scene` / `.project`
extensions" / "io-gltf ... have zero call sites") as if they were
current, wasting an audit cycle.

ISSUE-247's audit Q3 already flagged `:588-592` as partially stale at
the gap-1 level; this dispatch records the corrected current state in
a prepended subsection without retroactively editing the dated
2026-05-21 subsection (forward-only pattern per ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245
snapshots, ISSUE-251 BASELINE.md precedent).

## Scope

### MAY edit

- `plans/BASELINE.md` - prepend a new dated subsection
  `### 2026-05-28 - Editor-usability reconciliation (post-ISSUE-225 +
  ISSUE-249)` IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the existing `### 2026-05-21 - initial
  editor-usability adoption snapshot` subsection at approximately
  `:561`. The 2026-05-21 subsection (from `:561` through the end of
  the Editor-usability preflight section at approximately `:639`) MUST
  be preserved byte-identical; reconciliation is by addition, not by
  in-place edit.
- `Status.md` - prepend a forward-only snapshot above the existing
  most-recent snapshot, matching the ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245 / ISSUE-251
  precedent.
- `HANDOFF.md` - parallel snapshot prepended above the existing
  most-recent snapshot.
- `change.md` - insert a 2026-05-28 entry after the ISSUE-251
  2026-05-28 entry and before the 2026-05-23 15:20 REPO TRANSFER entry,
  matching the ISSUE-245 / ISSUE-251 insertion pattern.

### MAY add new files

- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-253_*.md`
- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-253_*.meta.json`
- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-253*.md`

### MUST NOT edit

- Any Rust source, test, bench, or fixture under `crates/**`,
  `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, `tools/**`, or `editor/**`.
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, or any `**/Cargo.toml`.
- Any other file under `plans/**` - specifically `plans/PLAN.md`,
  `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `plans/fileandfolderstructure.md`,
  `plans/README.md`.
- Any ADR under `docs/adr/**`.
- Any other subsection of `plans/BASELINE.md` (W03 / W04 / W08 / W10 /
  6.3 / 13.2 / Live-inspector wiring preflight / CommandBus
  integration design preflight) - every subsection OTHER than the new
  prepended 2026-05-28 subsection inside the Editor-usability preflight
  section stays byte-identical.
- The 2026-05-21 "initial editor-usability adoption snapshot"
  subsection itself (from `:561` through the end of the Editor-usability
  preflight section) - preserved verbatim. In particular: the
  "Cross-cutting pattern" prose at `:594`, the "Rejected/boundedness
  note - F - SpawnCuboidAt proposal" at `:596-607`, the "Status: PHASE
  9 PREFLIGHT" line at `:609`, the "Revisit triggers" at `:615-620`,
  and the "Notes / caveats" at `:622-639` - all preserved verbatim, even
  where the new 2026-05-28 subsection above notes their framing has
  partially aged.
- Architecture-lint source, fixtures, snapshots, exemptions.
- Any workflow file under `.github/workflows/**`.
- `Invoke-AiDispatch*.ps1`, `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`,
  `Get-AiDispatch*.ps1`, `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`, `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`,
  `new-handoff.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`,
  `.ai/*.schema.json`.
- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`, `ai_handoffs/templates/**`, any
  prior `ai_handoffs/*` packet or sidecar, any prior
  `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`.

### MUST NOT add new files

- No new Rust source / test / bench / fixture / crate / Cargo manifest.
- No new ADR, architecture-lint file, automation script, schema, or
  workflow file.
- No new doc file under `plans/`, `docs/`, or repo root.
- No new `.github/` files.

## Deliverables

- New `### 2026-05-28 - Editor-usability reconciliation (post-ISSUE-225
  + ISSUE-249)` subsection prepended above the 2026-05-21 subsection in
  the `## Editor-usability preflight (Phase 9)` section. The new
  subsection MUST:
  - State the headline current reality: load paths exist for `.glb`
    (`--glb`, Dispatch G predating this session), `.rge-project` /
    `.rge-scene` (`--scene`, ISSUE-225 commit `598f27e`); `--scene`
    produces a visible window with egui dock + Inspector chrome painted
    (ISSUE-249, main `007635d`); SAVE direction remains absent;
    Command Bus from editor UI remains unreached; `io-image` +
    `MenuRegistry` wiring + drag-drop remain absent.
  - Enumerate the specific stale lines with their current status:
    - `:588` Gap 1 - PARTIALLY STALE: load wired (`--glb` for `.glb`;
      `--scene` for `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene`); SAVE still absent;
      `no open_file / load_project / save_project symbols` claim STALE
      for `load_*`, STILL TRUE for `save_*`; zero-extension-match
      claim STALE (`.rge-project` and `.rge-scene` recognized at
      `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:213-214`); "the editor never
      calls [RON serialization]" claim STALE for deserialization, STILL
      TRUE for serialization; "hardcoded `CuboidOp(1.0, 1.0, 1.0)` is
      the only shape that ever exists" claim STALE; "authoring loop
      structurally unmeasurable" - PARTIALLY STALE (load loop now
      measurable; full save/load cycle still not).
    - `:590` Gap 2 - UNCHANGED: Command Bus remains structurally
      unreachable from the editor UI; no `KeyboardInput` keyboard
      branches added; `lifecycle.rs` catch-all on keyboard events
      stands.
    - `:592` Gap 3 - PARTIALLY STALE: `io-gltf::import_glb` IS now
      called via the `--glb` CLI path (Dispatch G); "no CLI argument
      parsing for a file path" claim STALE; `io-image::load_path` /
      `load_bytes` STILL have zero call sites from editor surface;
      `MenuRegistry::resolve` output STILL never acted upon; "no
      drag-drop path" UNCHANGED.
  - PRESERVE explicitly: the `:586` "Top 3 usability gaps (qualitative;
    baseline-state findings)" header itself, even though gaps 1 + 3
    have partially aged; the `:594-639` surrounding prose (Cross-cutting
    pattern / Rejected-boundedness / Status / Revisit triggers / Notes
    + caveats) in its entirety as dated history of the 2026-05-21
    posture; the "Workflows that work end-to-end TODAY" table at
    `:573-582` and the test-coverage paragraph at `:584` (still
    accurate).
  - Explicitly note: the `:594-620` Cross-cutting / Status / Revisit-
    triggers framing ("substrate complete in isolation, no user-input
    path connects to it") is partially stale on framing - CLI flag user-
    input paths (`--glb`, `--scene`) DO connect to visible scene
    changes - but the SPECIFIC revisit triggers at `:615-620` (a
    CommandBus integration design dispatch landing a decision, OR a
    non-CAD user-input path bound to a keyboard shortcut like Ctrl+S /
    Ctrl+O / PIE Play-Stop key) have NOT fired in their literal form.
    The reconciliation explicitly does NOT declare the triggers fired;
    the framing aging is recorded for the next audit, but the
    keyboard/menu trigger semantics stay binding. Full reconciliation
    of `:594-620` prose is OUT OF SCOPE for this dispatch - a separate
    chip can take it if/when an actual revisit trigger fires.
  - Cite closure evidence with file/line refs grounded at main
    `007635d`: `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:183-189` (`--glb` CLI
    parsing); `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:190-196` (`--scene` CLI
    parsing); `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:204` ("ISSUE-225" load
    helper banner) and the load helper body that follows;
    `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml:29-37` (the `rge-io-gltf` /
    `rge-data` / `rge-scene-loader` / `ron` editor-binary dep edges
    that supersede the stale `:592` "zero call sites" claim);
    `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:279-285` + `:313-328` +
    `:510-610` (ISSUE-249 init-split + egui-only render path that
    makes the `--scene` window visible); main commit reference
    `007635d` (post-ISSUE-249 / post-ISSUE-251 main HEAD).
- Forward-only snapshots in `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md`
  noting the reconciliation, matching the ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245 /
  ISSUE-251 forward-only snapshot precedent. Older dated snapshots
  preserved verbatim.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `plans/BASELINE.md` diff: pure prepend at approximately `:561`. The
  2026-05-21 subsection (`:561` through end of the Editor-usability
  preflight section pre-edit, shifted by N lines post-edit) is
  byte-identical to the pre-edit version. Verify with
  `git diff -- plans/BASELINE.md` showing only additions before
  `### 2026-05-21 - initial editor-usability adoption snapshot`.
- Every other subsection of `plans/BASELINE.md` (W03 / W04 / W08 / W10 /
  6.3 / 13.2 / Live-inspector wiring preflight / CommandBus
  integration design preflight) is byte-identical to the pre-edit
  version.
- The Editor-usability preflight section's framing paragraphs above
  the 2026-05-21 subsection (Budget anchors, "This entry is a Phase 9
  PREFLIGHT" paragraph, Methodology) are byte-identical to the
  pre-edit version. Only the new dated subsection is added.
- The new 2026-05-28 subsection contains all of: headline current
  reality, specific-line stale enumeration (gap 1 + gap 2 + gap 3 with
  their PARTIALLY-STALE / UNCHANGED / PARTIALLY-STALE statuses
  respectively), explicit "what to preserve" including the framing
  aging in `:594-620`, the explicit non-firing of literal revisit
  triggers, closure evidence with file/line refs grounded at main
  `007635d`, and forward-only-pattern reference to ISSUE-243 /
  ISSUE-245 / ISSUE-251 precedent.
- `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md` snapshots forward-only; older
  dated content byte-identical. `change.md` entry inserted at the
  ISSUE-251-pattern location (after the ISSUE-251 2026-05-28 entry and
  before the 2026-05-23 15:20 REPO TRANSFER entry).
- The Executor's `EXECUTION_REPORT` `change.md` paragraph wording
  matches the corrected ISSUE-251 wording: "NO standalone cargo
  commands outside `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`; NO `cargo bench`, no
  standalone `cargo test`, no fresh interactive editor run." (NOT the
  earlier "NO Cargo command run" wording that ISSUE-251's manual
  correction fixed.)
- No edit to any file outside the MAY-edit envelope.
- `git diff --check` - exit 0, no whitespace errors.
- `git status --short --branch` final - tracked changes confined to the
  MAY-edit envelope.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` - exit 0.

## Constraints / Non-Goals

- This dispatch does NOT reconcile the surrounding prose at
  `plans/BASELINE.md:594-620` (Cross-cutting pattern / Rejected-
  boundedness / Status / Revisit triggers). The new 2026-05-28
  subsection notes that prose's framing has partially aged, but full
  reconciliation requires either a revisit trigger to fire OR a
  separate hygiene chip explicitly scoped to that prose. Mentioning
  the aging in the new subsection's prose is acceptable; re-snapshotting
  it is OUT OF SCOPE.
- This dispatch does NOT declare the literal revisit triggers at `:615-
  620` fired. Neither trigger (CommandBus integration design dispatch
  landing a decision; non-CAD user-input path bound to keyboard
  shortcut) has fired in literal form. CLI flag user-input paths
  (`--glb` / `--scene`) connect to visible scene changes but are NOT
  keyboard / menu user-input paths in the trigger's literal sense.
  The "defer all user-facing editor wire-up dispatches" guidance at
  `:620` remains binding.
- This dispatch does NOT reconcile any other plans doc (`plans/PLAN.md`,
  `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `plans/fileandfolderstructure.md`,
  `plans/README.md`).
- This dispatch does NOT add new measurement, benchmark, or test
  results. No `cargo bench`, no `cargo test`, no fresh sample.
- This dispatch does NOT introduce new architectural claims, new pass /
  fail thresholds, or new revisit triggers.
- This dispatch does NOT change the publication path: PR-mode queue
  publication; no direct `origin/main` push; no auto-close of the
  GitHub issue.

## Verification Gates

The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
`EXECUTION_REPORT`:

- `git status --short --branch` before any edit - expected clean on the
  dispatch branch.
- `git diff -- plans/BASELINE.md` after edit - expected: pure additions
  before `### 2026-05-21 - initial editor-usability adoption snapshot`;
  ZERO changes to lines from `:561` through end of the Editor-usability
  preflight section (pre-edit numbering); ZERO changes to any other
  subsection.
- `git diff -- Status.md HANDOFF.md change.md` after edit - expected:
  pure prepend (Status, HANDOFF) or single inserted entry (change at the
  ISSUE-251-pattern location); older dated content byte-identical.
- `git diff --check` - exit 0.
- `git status --short --branch` final - tracked changes confined to the
  four MAY-edit files (`plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`,
  `change.md`) plus this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-253_*` and
  `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-253*.md`.
- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` - exit 0.

No standalone cargo gates are required beyond `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
That canonical gate mirrors CI and may run fmt, architecture lints, and
workspace tests. Do not run additional cargo commands outside that gate
unless a specific reason is documented.

## Halt Conditions

The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`,
`NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 2` if any of the following occur:

- Reconciling `:586-592` would require any in-place edit to the
  2026-05-21 subsection (`:561` through end of the Editor-usability
  preflight section) rather than pure prepending above `:561`.
- Reconciling the gap 1 / 2 / 3 findings would require touching any
  other subsection of `plans/BASELINE.md` (W03 / W04 / W08 / W10 /
  6.3 / 13.2 / Live-inspector wiring preflight / CommandBus
  integration design preflight).
- The new 2026-05-28 subsection's closure-evidence citations would
  require re-running cargo / fresh measurement / fresh source
  inspection beyond reading current files at main HEAD (commit
  `007635d`).
- More than one follow-up dispatch would be required to make the
  reconciliation honest (e.g. the surrounding `:594-620` prose cannot
  be left as-is without itself becoming actively misleading after this
  snapshot lands).
- The Executor judges that any of the literal revisit triggers at
  `:615-620` HAVE in fact fired (i.e. concludes that CLI flag paths
  satisfy the trigger semantics). Trigger-firing is a higher-stakes
  determination that belongs in human arbitration, not in a docs-only
  reconciliation chip.
- Tracked files are dirty before any edit is attempted.

## Planner Notes

This dispatch is grounded in:
- ISSUE-247's audit Q3
  (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-247_EXEC_2026-05-28_14-53-12+0300.md`), which
  flagged `plans/BASELINE.md:588-592` gap 1 as PARTIALLY STALE.
- ISSUE-225's `--scene` load-path wiring (commit `598f27e`, referenced
  inline at `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs:204`).
- ISSUE-249's render-init split + egui-only `render_frame` path
  (merged into main `007635d`).
- ISSUE-251's `plans/BASELINE.md` Live-inspector-wiring reconciliation
  (merged into main `273d658`) - the directly-mirrored precedent for
  this chip's forward-only-prepend pattern, change.md insertion
  location, dispatch.verify.ps1 verification-gate wording, and "what to
  preserve verbatim" discipline.

This chip is the SECOND `plans/BASELINE.md` reconciliation chip in
this dispatch arc; if a third becomes warranted (e.g. for the
CommandBus integration design preflight section), the pattern is now
well-established.

Intended publication path: queue PR mode
(`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -PublishMode pr`). The dispatch branch is
pushed and a PR is opened for human review without merging to
`origin/main` and without closing the GitHub issue automatically.

Per the orchestrator dispatch semantics in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`:
TASK + EXEC packets only, plus an optional CORRECT under a correction
round. Missing `_REVIEW_` / `_CLOSEOUT_` packets is normal - the Codex
control review goes to the gitignored
`.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-253/codex.control.round0.json`, not a packet.

   (handoff substrate) from the 2026-05-21 snapshot's A/B/C/D table:
   `rge_editor_egui_host::InspectorHandoff` mirrors the canonical

3. **The `--scene` no-window gap is closed** by ISSUE-249 (commit lands
   on main `007635d`): `init_render_state` constructs window + surface +
   `EguiHost` + `InspectorHandoff` unconditionally; `render_frame` has
   an egui-only branch for world-only launches.

Without this reconciliation, the next Phase 9 audit will rediscover the
2026-05-21 snapshot's " DOES NOT EXIST" / "NOT READY for live
inspector-tab wiring" / "no post-cuboid UI pass" findings as if they
were current, wasting an audit cycle and confusing the planning
substrate. ISSUE-247's audit Q3 already enumerated the specific stale
lines; this dispatch records the current state in a prepended snapshot
without retroactively editing the dated 2026-05-21 entry (which stays
preserved as history per the forward-only pattern established by
ISSUE-243 / ISSUE-245).

## Scope

## Acceptance Criteria

- `plans/BASELINE.md` diff: pure prepend at approximately `:662`. The

## Verification Gates

The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their

No standalone cargo gates are required beyond `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
That canonical gate mirrors CI and may run fmt, architecture lints, and

unless a specific reason is documented.

## Halt Conditions

The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`,
`NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 2` if any of the following occur:

- Reconciling the egui-host findings would require touching any other

## Planner Notes

This dispatch is grounded in ISSUE-247's audit Q3
(`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-247_EXEC_2026-05-28_14-53-12+0300.md`), which
