title: Decide ComponentValue.type_id convention for typed simple-scene payloads
body:
## Context

ISSUE-158 / PR #159 recovered a read-only audit for typed component payloads in `golden-projects/simple-scene`.

The audit found exact candidate RON payload strings for `Transform`, `Camera`, `Light`, and `Visibility`, but only the `Transform` `ComponentValue.type_id` string is currently proven by repo fixtures:

- Proven: `"rge::components::Transform"`
- Candidate envelope convention:
  - `"rge::components::Camera"`
  - `"rge::components::Light"`
  - `"rge::components::Visibility"`
- Candidate Rust module-path convention:
  - `"rge_components_render::camera::Camera"`
  - `"rge_components_render::light::Light"`
  - `"rge_components_visibility::visibility::Visibility"`
- Future option: wait for `#[derive(Reflect)]` emitted `FQ_TYPE_NAME` / real `TYPE_ID` conventions.

## Decision Needed

Choose the canonical `ComponentValue.type_id` convention for raw scene envelopes before any golden simple-scene typed-component fixture is edited.

## Questions

1. Should simple-scene use rge-data envelope convention strings, Rust fully-qualified module paths, or wait for Reflect-backed names?
2. Should `Visibility` be explicit as `Visible` on the camera entity, or omitted until a future light/mesh entity needs it?
3. If using the envelope convention, should `Camera` pin `fov_y_radians: 1.0471976` for the schema-only fixture?

## Recommendation To Decide

Prefer the current rge-data envelope convention for schema fixtures:
`"rge::components::Camera"`, `"rge::components::Light"`, `"rge::components::Visibility"`.

Record that this is a scene-envelope convention, not a Rust module path. Reflect-backed runtime identity can supersede or bridge it later through an explicit migration.

## After Decision

Only then queue the next dispatch: add typed raw `ComponentValue` payloads to `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` and extend `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` with schema-only assertions.

That is the best next task because it resolves the one real blocker without letting the executor guess a convention.

title: [ai-dispatch] Broaden #18: TransformOp label preservation + stale-test reconciliation
body:
Follow-up to failed dispatch #18. The `TransformOp` label-preserving implementation on branch `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-18` (commit `6807cb6`) is conceptually sound, but #18's scope only permitted editing `transform.rs` — leaving tests elsewhere that still assert the old "strips labels" contract failing, so #18 could never pass verification.

## Goal

`TransformOp` preserves tessellation face labels, with every affected test reconciled.

## MAY edit

- `crates/cad-core/src/operators/transform.rs` — keep the label-preserving implementation; commit `6807cb6` is the reference. Positions transform; indices clone unchanged; labeled input uses `Tessellation::with_labels(..., labels.to_vec())`; unlabeled input stays on `Tessellation::new`; `output_is_labeled(&[true])` becomes `true`.
- `crates/cad-core/src/graph/operator_graph.rs` (~line 795) and `crates/cad-core/tests/labeled_tessellation_pipeline.rs` (~lines 163, 303) — update these stale tests, which still assert the OLD strip-labels contract.
- `crates/cad-core/tests/topology_lineage_smoke.rs` (~line 106) — this failed during #18's verification but does not use `TransformOp` and passes on current `main`. Rerun it; change it ONLY if this branch proves a genuine intended expectation change — otherwise diagnose the failure, do not blind-update the assertion.

## Verification

Run the full `cargo test` suite. Note: `cargo` is not on the default PATH in this workspace — prepend `A:\RustCache\cargo\bin` (CARGO_HOME = `A:\RustCache\cargo`). The earlier #18 executor wrongly reported an "environment limitation" because of this; it is not a real limitation.

## Notes

- #18 itself stays `ai-dispatch-failed` as the record of the narrow-scope attempt; this issue supersedes it.
- Do not merge or publish `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-18` / `6807cb6` as-is.
